title: Trigonostemon lanceolatus
intro: Trigonostemon lanceolatus, also known as Lance-leaved Trigonostemon, is a plant species within the family Euphorbiaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Bioactive constituents of Trigonostemon lanceolatus include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, and is commonly used in the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Trigonostemon lanceolatus is a species of plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. This family is commonly known as the spurge family. The Euphorbiaceae family consists of a diverse group of flowering plants, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plants within this family are often herbaceous or shrub-like and feature milky sap. They are commonly found in a variety of habitats, including forests and grasslands.
